Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-MN03-RXCM-30BW-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR UPDATES: INDUSTRY LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR UPDATES: INDUSTRY LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ WRI & ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION: EARLY CLIMATE HEALTH INVESTMENTS GENERATE 68-FOLD GAINS IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

New World Resources Institute analysis, supported by The Rockefeller Foundation, reveals that every $1 invested in preparing for climate-caused health risks can yield up to $68 in benefits for communities in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Middle East

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION WRI & ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION: EARLY CLIMATE HEALTH INVESTMENTS GENERATE 68-FOLD GAINS IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

WASHINGTON, D.C. | May 6, 2026 - World Resources Institute (WRI) and The Rockefeller Foundation today announced new research from WRI, which finds that early investment in climate health solutions in low- and middle-income countries can yield between US$4 and US$68 in benefits for every US$1 invested.

With funding from The Rockefeller Foundation, WRI analyzed a range of tools and services - such as early warning systems, disease surveillance and public awareness campaigns - across 46 projects in 40 countries in Sub-Saharan Africa (26), Latin America and the Caribbean (7), Asia (4), and the Middle East and North Africa (3).

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION DENGUEAI: NEW MODEL THAT ANTICIPATES OUTBREAKS WITH 93% EFFECTIVENESS AND THREE WEEKS' ADVANCE NOTICE

Cali, Colombia | May 4, 2026 - The City of Santiago de Cali, Universidad Icesi, and The Rockefeller Foundation today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) aimed at reducing the incidence of dengue in the city and strengthening preparedness and response capacities for future epidemics. This collaboration grows out of The Rockefeller Foundation's support for public health work and seeks to protect public health and advance sustainable solutions to one of the most significant public health challenges in both the department of Valle del Cauca and Colombia more broadly. The initiative, known as Dengue.AI (Dengue.IA in Spanish), brings together scientific expertise, technological development, and innovation through a cooperative model that integrates philanthropy, academia, and the public sector.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJG-9DP3-RRX6-70T6-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT NEWS FROM PERKINS COIE AND OTHER PEERS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:58ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT NEWS FROM PERKINS COIE AND OTHER PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY PREVAILS IN WINERY DISPUTE AFTER FIVE-DAY JURY TRIAL

Ateam of Pillsbury lawyers led by Austin-based Litigation partner Casey Low prevailed in a five-day jury trial in favor of client Fred Schrader, the founder of the highly acclaimed Schrader Cellars winery, and the winery's current owner.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ KING & SPALDING STEPHEN VAUGHN NAMED ONE OF D.C.S 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE BY WASHINGTONIAN MAGAZINE

King &Spalding Partner Stephen Vaughn has been named one of Washington, D.C.'s 500 Most Influential People by Washingtonian magazine.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP CHRISTINE WONG NAMED TO SAN FRANCISCO BUSINESS TIMES 2026 MOST INFLUENTIAL WOMEN IN BAY AREA BUSINESS

The San Francisco Business Times has named Morrison Foerster partner Christine Wong to its 2026 list of the Most Influential Women in Bay Area Business. This recognition honors trailblazing leaders who are shaping industries, championing mentorship and community impact, and redefining what leadership looks like across the region.

Morrison Foerster, a leading global law firm, is pleased to announce the addition of three partners, Mike Pierides, Will Holder, and Oliver Bell, adding significant strength and depth to the firm's global, cross‑border technology transactions credentials. Collectively, they bring over 55 years of experience bolstering the firm's capabilities in tech-focused M&A, complex technology transactions, outsourcing, and digital transformation.

Morrison Foerster's State and Local Government Task Force is pleased to provide our quarterly newsletter summarizing noteworthy developments from state attorneys general ("State AGs") across the country and local government agencies and legislative bodies, with links to primary sources. + GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [5 Press Releases]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS SEASONED FINANCE AND RESTRUCTURING SHAREHOLDER GERARD C. MARTIN IN CHICAGO

CHICAGO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added secured finance attorney Gerard C. Martin to its Chicago office as a shareholder in the firm's Restructuring & Special Situations Practice. He joins the firm from Reed Smith LLP.

Martin brings deep experience across the full spectrum of financing transactions and debt restructurings. He represents lenders, borrowers, sponsors, and investors in a broad range of U.S. and international financing transactions, including cash flow and asset-based loans, leveraged loans, acquisition financings, bridge facilities, recapitalizations, and complex intercreditor arrangements, including unitranche and multitranche structures. He regularly advises on large, syndicated credit facilities and cross-border and multicurrency financings. Martin also maintains an active equipment and aviation finance practice, representing parties in aircraft and engine financings, leveraged leases, rolling stock transactions, and project finance matters. He works with clients across a wide range of industries, including manufacturing, energy, aviation, technology, health care, and financial services, among others.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISES SELLER ON HYPERSCALE DATA CENTER TRANSACTION IN JAPAN

TOKYO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented the seller in the disposition of a stabilized hyperscale data center located in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The transaction is among the largest asset‑level sales of a fully leased, operational hyperscale data center in the Japanese market to date.

The approximately 40‑megawatt facility is leased to a single U.S. hyperscale cloud provider. The seller is a joint venture between a fund sponsored by a U.S. institutional manager and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company, and the buyer is a consortium comprising Singapore‑listed CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, which acquired a 49% interest, and a fund managed by an affiliate of the Japanese trading company, which acquired the remaining 51%.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISED REX CONCEPTS AND MCWIN ON THE INITIAL PUBLIC OFFERING AND LISTING ON THE WARSAW STOCK EXCHANGE

WARSAW -7 May 2026- Greenberg Traurig advised Rex Concepts S.A. (the "Company"), the master franchisee of Burger King and Popeyes in Poland, Czechia and Romania, as well as Rex Invest CEE - a company in the McWin group - as the selling shareholder, on the initial public offering (IPO) of the Company's shares and the admission of the shares to trading on the regulated market operated by the Warsaw Stock Exchange.

The total value of the offering amounted to approximately PLN 497.8 million, with a final offer price set at PLN 14 per share. The offering comprised 32 million newly issued and 3,555,555 existing shares offered by Rex Invest CEE in connection with the stabilisation structure. Gross proceeds from the new issue amounted to approximately PLN 448 million.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP MATHEW ROSENGART NAMED AMONG THE 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE IN LOS ANGELES BY THE LOS ANGELES BUSINESS JOURNAL FOR FIFTH CONSECUTIVE YEAR

LOS ANGELES - May 7, 2026 - Mathew S. Rosengart, co-chair of the Media & Entertainment Litigation Practice of global law firmGreenberg Traurig, LLP, was again named to the Los Angeles Business Journal's "LA500" list. According to the publication, the list honors the 500 most influential people in Los Angeles, based on their varied achievements in the community.

Rosengart, a former Justice Department trial attorney, has been heralded as "Hollywood's King of Litigators" by Variety, called a "globally recognized legal eagle" by The Hollywood Reporter, acclaimed as a "household name" by Billboard and the Los Angeles Business Journal, which referred to him as a "trusted counsellor" who is "widely recognized as one of the nation's preeminent litigators," and recognized as a "high powered" lawyer by The New York Times. He has twice been honored by Forbes in its list of the Top 200 lawyers in the United States, which referred to him as a "revered" litigator and, according to the publication, recognizes the "finest in the field," whose "integrity," "expertise, passion, and purpose set them apart" as "the best in the business."

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG EXPANDS GLOBAL HOSPITALITY PRACTICE WITH STRATEGIC HIRES IN PHOENIX AND MEXICO

NEW YORK - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has strengthened its market-leading Real Estate and Hospitality practices with the addition of two seasoned cross-border attorneys focused on hospitality, real estate, and international matters. Carlos A. Sugich joins as co-chair of the Americas Hospitality Group in Phoenix and Mexico, and Carlos Freaner joins as a shareholder in Mexico. They arrive from Snell & Wilmer.

Building on the strength of Greenberg Traurig's 70-plus-attorney Mexico City office, the firm is expanding its cross-border capabilities in Mexico and key hospitality markets with a new presence in Los Cabos, where Sugich will serve as managing shareholder. He led the launch of his prior firm's Los Cabos office nearly two decades ago.

Source: Company Website

The Intellectual Property team advised Pharmakon Advisors on entering into a five-year, up to $250 million senior secured debt facility with Zenas BioPharma, who are developing a bifunctional monoclonal antibody drug product for the treatment of IgG4-related disease (IgG4-RD).

+ GOODWIN GUIDES HUMANS& IN RAISING OVER $500 MILLION AT $4.5 BILLION VALUATION IN SEED ROUND

The Technology team guided humans& in raising over $500 million in a seed financing round, which values the company at approximately $4.5 billion. The round was led by SV Angel and co-founder Georges Harik, while Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, and Alphabet's VC arm, GV, among other venture capital investors, also participated.

Source : Company Website

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

Goodwin congratulates Flow Neuroscience on receiving FDA approval of their breakthrough at-home brain-stimulation device for the treatment of major depressive disorder (MDD). FDA approval marks the first time clinicians in the US are able to prescribe an at-home non-drug therapy as both a stand-alone and an adjunctive treatment for adults with moderate to severe depression. Goodwin's Technology and Life Sciences teams were thrilled to assist Flow Neuroscience in bringing this breakthrough device to patients in the US

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP AXELERA AI SECURES OVER $250M IN RECORD EU AI SEMICONDUCTOR INVESTMENT

Axelera AI, an Edge AI hardware startup, has raised more than $250 million in its most recent funding round.

Orrick advised Axelera AI on the transaction.

THE COMPANY Axelera AI is a European semiconductor leader developing advanced AI acceleration hardware for edge computing and data centre applications, driving the next generation of artificial intelligence across Europe.

Source : Company Website

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.

Source : Company Website

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLPREALTEK SECURES KEY ANTITRUST WIN IN SMART TV CHIP DISPUTE

Semiconductor company Realtek Semiconductor Corp. secured a key win in its dispute over a "bounty" patent licensing agreement by competitors that included a $1 million incentive to sue Realtek with the District Court of the Northern District of California denying the defendants' early motion for summary judgment. American Lawyer recognized the win in its Litigators of the Week column.

urn:contentItem:6JJK-N943-S0TN-F3TS-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT NEWS OF PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:11ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT NEWS OF PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NAIROBI HOSTS ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S 60TH ANNIVERSARY, EMPHASIZING LOCAL LEADERSHIP AND CAPITAL ACTIVATION

NAIROBI — The Rockefeller Foundation marked the 60th anniversary of its Africa Regional Office, founded in Nairobi in 1966, on the 1st of May, 2026, as part of AfricaXchange 2026, a forum to mobilize capital, policy influence, and scalable solutions for Africa’s challenges. The gathering drew more than 400 leaders from diverse sectors and underscored the Foundation’s enduring commitment to human capital, health, agriculture, and energy across the continent.William Asiko, head of the Africa office, said development finance is changing as new players enter the field. "African philanthropy, private capital, and regional institutions are reshaping not just how development is financed, but how it is delivered." His remarks came amid OECD data showing a decline in official development assistance, reinforcing the need for new models of investment and stronger partnerships that allow Africa to shape its own destiny.Elizabeth Yee, Executive Vice President of Programs, closed the event by stressing collective action. "Philanthropy’s job is not to be the hero. It’s to take risk others can’t, build trust where it’s been missing, and move countries from ambition to execution." The conference theme, "Money, Markets, and Mindsets," framed an approach to finance and lead Africa with African leadership. The Rockefeller Foundation also launched the Africa Big Bets Fellows, a class of ten changemakers advancing community-led solutions across several countries.The gathering highlighted cross-sector work to activate capital and policy levers for Africa’s self-determination, including discussions on energy transition, climate-resilient agriculture and health system reform. Notable attendees included E. Prof. Anyang’ Nyong’o, Governor of Kisumu County, and E. Nardos Bekele-Thomas, CEO of the African Union Development Agency.

+ FIRST COHORT OF AFRICA BIG BETS FELLOWS TARGET ENERGY, HEALTH AND CLIMATE GOALS

The Rockefeller Foundation has launched the Africa Big Bets Fellows at AfricaXchange in Nairobi, presenting a cohort of ten changemakers from six countries. These fellows will drive community-led projects across critical zones such as energy access, food security, healthcare, financial inclusion, climate resilience, and migration, over a five-month program.The program's timing and scale reflect Africa's ongoing development challenges and opportunities. The launch occurs on 28th of April, 2026, marking the 60th anniversary of the Africa Regional Office.Projects include mobile healthcare in rural Ghana, waste-to-resource systems for cleaner cities, regenerative agriculture tied to school feeding in Kenya, AI-powered farming in Malawi, and expanded financial services in Nigeria. The initiative sits within AfricaXchange to connect funders, practitioners, and policymakers and help shift development to locally led models.

+ FOUNDATION SIGNALS STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN RESKILLING AS AI ADVANCES

The Rockefeller Foundation on 28th April 2026 unveiled a grant-based nationwide effort to address AI-related disruption in the job market, aiming to prevent displacement and guide workers toward roles that complement automation. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development and places emphasis on program design, metrics and evaluation, with plans to measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to gauge the impact of automation. Funding will be provided through grants, pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The organization notes that implementation choices will determine whether the initiative yields scalable reskilling pipelines or a patchwork of pilot projects. Viewed as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative, the foundation hopes to catalyze new models of retraining and public-private collaboration, while contributing to policy discussions on workforce development such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It anticipates a rising demand for data-driven evaluations in the skills and labor analytics ecosystem as the program unfolds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES HOMETOWN FUND AIMED AT DRIVING COMMUNITY RENEWAL IN ROSEBURG

ROSEBURG, Ore. – The Ford Foundation unveiled the Hometown Fund, a $10 million, decade-long effort to revitalize Roseburg, offering capital grants up to $1 million and planning grants up to $75,000 to support large, community-driven projects and cross-sector collaboration.“So I really, really hope that the grown-ups can work together to build tangible spaces and places where kids can say that they are loved and that there’s a future here,” said Kara Inae Carlisle, President and CEO of the Ford Foundation. She highlighted the goal of lasting, meaningful change over the next ten years and a commitment to helping families thrive. The initiative builds on the foundation’s broad charitable legacy of about $810 million since inception.Historically, the foundation has focused on education, early childhood development, and community economic development. Its Ford Scholars Program currently supports roughly 1,000 students annually. The foundation has also contributed to regional crisis recovery, including collaboration with the Oregon Community Foundation and Meyer Memorial Trust to distribute $10 million for rebuilding after the 2020 Labor Day wildfires.Interested organizations should start by filling out an online information form that outlines their project’s scope and impact. After submission, Ford staff will review the proposal and schedule a meeting with the applicant. Projects meeting the fund’s goals of multi-sector collaboration and large-scale transformation will be invited to submit a formal application.

+ NHRC APPLAUDS FORD FOUNDATION'S LONGSTANDING PARTNERSHIP IN NIGERIAN HUMAN RIGHTS WORK

On Apr. 28, 2026, the NHRC in Abuja recognized the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its long-running support to human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony brought together Commission executives and was attended by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu lauded the Foundation for backing efforts to expand access to justice and to strengthen the Commission's governance and operations. He noted that tackling Nigeria's human rights challenges requires sustained cooperation with development partners to translate policy into practical outcomes for citizens.Aniagolu-Okoye said the recognition underscores the Foundation's ongoing commitment to both awareness and practical justice. She emphasized that rights education must be paired with strong enforcement mechanisms, and she highlighted the Foundation's role in supporting citizens to seek remedy when rights are violated.The event underscored the value of continuing public-private collaboration, with Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, acknowledging the MOVE project and the dashboard system as key areas advanced with Foundation support.

+ SOFT POWER OF ECOWAS FIRST LADIES HERALDED AS KEY TO GBV REFORMS, FORD FOUNDATION WARNS

The Ford Foundation urged First Ladies at the ECOWAS Forum on 24th April 2026 to harness their influence to combat gender-based violence, arguing that the soft power of First Ladies can drive social change and strengthen accountability.It highlighted the continued prevalence of GBV, noting that one in three women globally are affected and that child marriage remains a concern in West and Central Africa, with many girls married before 18.Economic costs were cited as a compelling reason for action, with GBV-related losses reaching up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, surpassing investments in some priority sectors such as education.While First Ladies are not formal policymakers, the Ford Foundation stressed their essential role in shaping public discourse and decision-making, pointing to Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as leaders whose campaigns elevated women’s issues and prompted national responses. Such examples demonstrate how strategic use of soft power can catalyze systemic change.The Foundation reiterated ongoing investments in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, including partnerships with First Ladies and governors’ spouses to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based services, and challenge harmful norms. It emphasized that laws alone are insufficient without public trust and accessible support for survivors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY PREVAILS IN WINERY DISPUTE AFTER FIVE-DAY JURY TRIAL

Ateam of Pillsbury lawyers led by Austin-based Litigation partner Casey Low prevailed in a five-day jury trial in favor of client Fred Schrader, the founder of the highly acclaimed Schrader Cellars winery, and the winery's current owner.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ KING & SPALDING STEPHEN VAUGHN NAMED ONE OF D.C.S 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE BY WASHINGTONIAN MAGAZINE

King &Spalding Partner Stephen Vaughn has been named one of Washington, D.C.'s 500 Most Influential People by Washingtonian magazine.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [5 Press Releases]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS SEASONED FINANCE AND RESTRUCTURING SHAREHOLDER GERARD C. MARTIN IN CHICAGO

CHICAGO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added secured finance attorney Gerard C. Martin to its Chicago office as a shareholder in the firm's Restructuring & Special Situations Practice. He joins the firm from Reed Smith LLP.

Martin brings deep experience across the full spectrum of financing transactions and debt restructurings. He represents lenders, borrowers, sponsors, and investors in a broad range of U.S. and international financing transactions, including cash flow and asset-based loans, leveraged loans, acquisition financings, bridge facilities, recapitalizations, and complex intercreditor arrangements, including unitranche and multitranche structures. He regularly advises on large, syndicated credit facilities and cross-border and multicurrency financings. Martin also maintains an active equipment and aviation finance practice, representing parties in aircraft and engine financings, leveraged leases, rolling stock transactions, and project finance matters. He works with clients across a wide range of industries, including manufacturing, energy, aviation, technology, health care, and financial services, among others.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISES SELLER ON HYPERSCALE DATA CENTER TRANSACTION IN JAPAN

TOKYO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented the seller in the disposition of a stabilized hyperscale data center located in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The transaction is among the largest asset‑level sales of a fully leased, operational hyperscale data center in the Japanese market to date.

The approximately 40‑megawatt facility is leased to a single U.S. hyperscale cloud provider. The seller is a joint venture between a fund sponsored by a U.S. institutional manager and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company, and the buyer is a consortium comprising Singapore‑listed CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, which acquired a 49% interest, and a fund managed by an affiliate of the Japanese trading company, which acquired the remaining 51%.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISED REX CONCEPTS AND MCWIN ON THE INITIAL PUBLIC OFFERING AND LISTING ON THE WARSAW STOCK EXCHANGE

WARSAW -7 May 2026- Greenberg Traurig advised Rex Concepts S.A. (the "Company"), the master franchisee of Burger King and Popeyes in Poland, Czechia and Romania, as well as Rex Invest CEE - a company in the McWin group - as the selling shareholder, on the initial public offering (IPO) of the Company's shares and the admission of the shares to trading on the regulated market operated by the Warsaw Stock Exchange.

The total value of the offering amounted to approximately PLN 497.8 million, with a final offer price set at PLN 14 per share. The offering comprised 32 million newly issued and 3,555,555 existing shares offered by Rex Invest CEE in connection with the stabilisation structure. Gross proceeds from the new issue amounted to approximately PLN 448 million.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP MATHEW ROSENGART NAMED AMONG THE 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE IN LOS ANGELES BY THE LOS ANGELES BUSINESS JOURNAL FOR FIFTH CONSECUTIVE YEAR

LOS ANGELES - May 7, 2026 - Mathew S. Rosengart, co-chair of the Media & Entertainment Litigation Practice of global law firmGreenberg Traurig, LLP, was again named to the Los Angeles Business Journal's "LA500" list. According to the publication, the list honors the 500 most influential people in Los Angeles, based on their varied achievements in the community.

Rosengart, a former Justice Department trial attorney, has been heralded as "Hollywood's King of Litigators" by Variety, called a "globally recognized legal eagle" by The Hollywood Reporter, acclaimed as a "household name" by Billboard and the Los Angeles Business Journal, which referred to him as a "trusted counsellor" who is "widely recognized as one of the nation's preeminent litigators," and recognized as a "high powered" lawyer by The New York Times. He has twice been honored by Forbes in its list of the Top 200 lawyers in the United States, which referred to him as a "revered" litigator and, according to the publication, recognizes the "finest in the field," whose "integrity," "expertise, passion, and purpose set them apart" as "the best in the business."

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG EXPANDS GLOBAL HOSPITALITY PRACTICE WITH STRATEGIC HIRES IN PHOENIX AND MEXICO

NEW YORK - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has strengthened its market-leading Real Estate and Hospitality practices with the addition of two seasoned cross-border attorneys focused on hospitality, real estate, and international matters. Carlos A. Sugich joins as co-chair of the Americas Hospitality Group in Phoenix and Mexico, and Carlos Freaner joins as a shareholder in Mexico. They arrive from Snell & Wilmer.

Building on the strength of Greenberg Traurig's 70-plus-attorney Mexico City office, the firm is expanding its cross-border capabilities in Mexico and key hospitality markets with a new presence in Los Cabos, where Sugich will serve as managing shareholder. He led the launch of his prior firm's Los Cabos office nearly two decades ago.

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

Goodwin congratulates Flow Neuroscience on receiving FDA approval of their breakthrough at-home brain-stimulation device for the treatment of major depressive disorder (MDD). FDA approval marks the first time clinicians in the US are able to prescribe an at-home non-drug therapy as both a stand-alone and an adjunctive treatment for adults with moderate to severe depression. Goodwin's Technology and Life Sciences teams were thrilled to assist Flow Neuroscience in bringing this breakthrough device to patients in the US

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.
