PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION MARKS SIX DECADES IN NAIROBI, UNVEILS AFRICA-FOCUSED INVESTMENTS AND FELLOWS

NAIROBI — The Rockefeller Foundation marked the 60th anniversary of its Africa Regional Office, established in Nairobi in 1966, on the 1st of May, 2026. The milestone was celebrated during AfricaXchange 2026, a forum designed to catalyze systemic reform by uniting leaders who can deploy capital, influence policy and scale solutions to Africa's most pressing problems. The event, which drew more than 400 leaders from government, business and civil society, reaffirmed the Foundation's century-long commitment to investing in human capital, health, agriculture and energy across the continent.William Asiko, Senior Vice President and head of the Africa office, emphasized that development finance is evolving as new players emerge. "African philanthropy, private capital, and regional institutions are reshaping not just how development is financed, but how it is delivered," he said. The remarks followed OECD data showing a sharp decline in official development assistance, underscoring the urgency of new models that blend philanthropy, public funds and private investment to empower African nations as equal partners in their growth.Elizabeth Yee, Executive Vice President of Programs, closed the conference by noting that collective action matters. "Philanthropy’s job is not to be the hero. It’s to take risk others can’t, build trust where it’s been missing, and move countries from ambition to execution," she stated. The core theme, "Money, Markets, and Mindsets," sought to ensure Africa's future is built and led by Africans. The Foundation also introduced the inaugural Africa Big Bets Fellows, a cohort of ten changemakers driving community-centered solutions in multiple countries.AfricaXchange 2026 advanced discussions on activating capital and policy levers to support self-determination. Delegates explored energy transition, climate-resilient agriculture and health systems reform, with participation from figures such as E. Prof. Anyang’ Nyong’o, Governor of Kisumu County, and E. Nardos Bekele-Thomas, CEO of the African Union Development Agency, highlighting the need for cross-sector collaboration to sustain resilience and growth.Beyond this year’s event, the Foundation’s Africa work traces back to early initiatives in the region, including the 1925 West Africa Yellow Fever Commission. Today, roughly one third of The Rockefeller Foundation’s global funding is allocated to Africa, targeting energy abundance, food security, health and innovation. The Foundation continues to empower local solutions and strengthen partnerships to forge a more prosperous future for the continent.

+ AFRICAXCHANGE DEBUTS INAUGURAL AFRICA BIG BETS FELLOWS TO SCALE COMMUNITY-LED INNOVATION

The Rockefeller Foundation unveiled the Africa Big Bets Fellows at its AfricaXchange gathering in Nairobi, naming ten fellows from Ghana, Kenya, Malawi, Nigeria, South Africa, and Tanzania who will lead community-driven responses to pressing development challenges. The five-month program aims to scale ideas across energy access, food security, healthcare, financial inclusion, climate resilience, and migration.The announcement comes on 28th of April, 2026, the 60th anniversary of the Foundation's Africa Regional Office. Leaders said the fellows represent a new generation of African innovators whose locally driven solutions are designed to expand opportunity, bolster resilience, and improve quality of life across health, agriculture, clean energy, and digital finance.Across Africa, structural headwinds persist, including limited electricity access for hundreds of millions and gaps in global climate finance. Yet the continent also offers significant potential for renewable energy development and sustainable growth if adequate support is provided.Projects span a broad range, from mobile healthcare in rural Ghana and waste-to-resource systems for cleaner cities to regenerative agriculture linked to school feeding in Kenya, AI-powered farming in Malawi, and expanded financial services in Nigeria.The program sits within AfricaXchange, a platform that brings funders, practitioners, and policymakers together to strengthen locally led development and connect innovators to global networks and resources to scale impact.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION LAUNCHES GRANTS-DRIVEN INITIATIVE TO MITIGATE AUTOMATION'S JOB DISPLACEMENT

On 28th April 2026, the Rockefeller Foundation outlined a bold push to counter AI-driven workforce disruption with a grant-based national initiative designed to prevent displacement and shift workers into AI-complementary roles. The Foundation emphasizes interventions for workers rather than product development, centering on program design, metrics and evaluation to track displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions caused by automation. Funding will flow through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments; decisions during implementation will matter for whether the effort yields scalable reskilling pipelines or fragmented pilots without lasting impact. As a strategic philanthropic signal, the move is not regulatory or corporate and aims to catalyze innovative retraining models and strengthened public-private collaboration, aligning with broader policy debates on universal basic income, sectoral training and apprenticeships. Stakeholders expect a growing emphasis on measurable, data-driven evaluations within skills and labor analytics, with close attention to funding priorities and partnership dynamics, including durable credentialing and employer engagement, as well as placement metrics.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROSEBURG TO BENEFIT FROM FORD FOUNDATION'S $10 MILLION COMMUNITY REVITALIZATION FUND

ROSEBURG, Ore. – The Ford Foundation announced the Hometown Fund, a $10 million investment to revitalize Roseburg over the next decade. The program will provide capital grants of up to $1 million and pre-development planning grants up to $75,000 to support large-scale, community-driven projects within the city limits, with cross-sector collaboration as a core objective. The announcement was made on 5th May 2026.“So I really, really hope that the grown-ups can work together to build tangible spaces and places where kids can say that they are loved and that there’s a future here,” said Kara Inae Carlisle, President and CEO of the Ford Foundation. She stressed the aim of meaningful, long-term improvement and a commitment to helping families thrive, noting the foundation’s long-standing history of charitable giving totaling about $810 million since its inception.Traditionally, the foundation has prioritized education, early childhood development, and community economic development. Its Ford Scholars Program currently supports about 1,000 students annually, equipping them with resources to pursue academic goals. The foundation has also supported regional crisis recovery, including a collaboration with the Oregon Community Foundation and Meyer Memorial Trust to distribute $10 million for rebuilding after the 2020 Labor Day wildfires.Organizations seeking funding for the Hometown Fund begin with an online information form describing their project’s scope and anticipated impact. Ford staff review submissions and contact applicants to schedule a meeting. Projects aligned with cross-sector collaboration and large-scale transformation will be invited to submit a formal application.The program signals a major commitment to Roseburg’s future and rural revitalization, aiming to benefit current residents and future generations.

+ FORD FOUNDATION AWARDED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO NIGERIA'S HUMAN RIGHTS AGENDA

On Apr. 28, 2026, in Abuja, the National Human Rights Commission honored the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its longstanding support of human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony drew senior NHRC officials and directors, and the honor was accepted by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.NHRC Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu praised the Foundation's steady backing, especially for initiatives that broaden access to justice and strengthen the Commission's capacity to protect rights. He stressed that Nigeria's human rights landscape is broad and complex, and that durable progress depends on ongoing partnerships with development partners rather than isolated action.Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as meaningful and encouraging, and reiterated the Foundation's commitment to equipping citizens with knowledge of their rights and support to pursue justice when those rights are violated. She warned that awareness alone is not enough without effective enforcement mechanisms and highlighted the Foundation's role in fostering stronger enforcement through collaboration and capacity building.The event also underscored the value of continued cooperation as the country confronts evolving human rights challenges. Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, expressed gratitude for the Ford Foundation's enduring partnership, citing the MOVE project and the Commission's dashboard system as notable advances that benefited from the Foundation's early support.

+ WEST AFRICA FIRST LADIES CALLED TO LEAD ON GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE, FORD FOUNDATION SAYS

At the ECOWAS First Ladies Forum on Zero Tolerance for GBV in Banjul on 24th April 2026, the Ford Foundation urged First Ladies across West Africa and the region to leverage their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence, framing the First Ladies' soft power as a catalyst for social change and stronger accountability.Aniagolu noted that despite existing frameworks and commitments, GBV remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with more than 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18.The Foundation highlighted the economic cost of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, a drain that rivals investments in key sectors such as education.The Ford Foundation stressed that First Ladies are not formal policymakers but can shape public discourse and influence decision-making across the continent. It cited Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as examples, noting that instruments like the Better Life for Rural Women Programme and Hands Off Our Girls campaign elevated women’s issues and contributed to national actions, underscoring the potential of soft power to drive systemic change.The Foundation has continued to invest in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, partnering with First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu argued that laws and policies alone are not enough without public trust and accessible services, pointing to persistent silence among survivors fueled by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHY-RN03-RXPP-F104-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NAIROBI HOSTS ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S 60TH ANNIVERSARY, EMPHASIZING LOCAL LEADERSHIP AND CAPITAL ACTIVATION

NAIROBI — The Rockefeller Foundation marked the 60th anniversary of its Africa Regional Office, founded in Nairobi in 1966, on the 1st of May, 2026, as part of AfricaXchange 2026, a forum to mobilize capital, policy influence, and scalable solutions for Africa’s challenges. The gathering drew more than 400 leaders from diverse sectors and underscored the Foundation’s enduring commitment to human capital, health, agriculture, and energy across the continent.William Asiko, head of the Africa office, said development finance is changing as new players enter the field. "African philanthropy, private capital, and regional institutions are reshaping not just how development is financed, but how it is delivered." His remarks came amid OECD data showing a decline in official development assistance, reinforcing the need for new models of investment and stronger partnerships that allow Africa to shape its own destiny.Elizabeth Yee, Executive Vice President of Programs, closed the event by stressing collective action. "Philanthropy’s job is not to be the hero. It’s to take risk others can’t, build trust where it’s been missing, and move countries from ambition to execution." The conference theme, "Money, Markets, and Mindsets," framed an approach to finance and lead Africa with African leadership. The Rockefeller Foundation also launched the Africa Big Bets Fellows, a class of ten changemakers advancing community-led solutions across several countries.The gathering highlighted cross-sector work to activate capital and policy levers for Africa’s self-determination, including discussions on energy transition, climate-resilient agriculture and health system reform. Notable attendees included E. Prof. Anyang’ Nyong’o, Governor of Kisumu County, and E. Nardos Bekele-Thomas, CEO of the African Union Development Agency.

+ FIRST COHORT OF AFRICA BIG BETS FELLOWS TARGET ENERGY, HEALTH AND CLIMATE GOALS

The Rockefeller Foundation has launched the Africa Big Bets Fellows at AfricaXchange in Nairobi, presenting a cohort of ten changemakers from six countries. These fellows will drive community-led projects across critical zones such as energy access, food security, healthcare, financial inclusion, climate resilience, and migration, over a five-month program.The program's timing and scale reflect Africa's ongoing development challenges and opportunities. The launch occurs on 28th of April, 2026, marking the 60th anniversary of the Africa Regional Office.Projects include mobile healthcare in rural Ghana, waste-to-resource systems for cleaner cities, regenerative agriculture tied to school feeding in Kenya, AI-powered farming in Malawi, and expanded financial services in Nigeria. The initiative sits within AfricaXchange to connect funders, practitioners, and policymakers and help shift development to locally led models.

+ FOUNDATION SIGNALS STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN RESKILLING AS AI ADVANCES

The Rockefeller Foundation on 28th April 2026 unveiled a grant-based nationwide effort to address AI-related disruption in the job market, aiming to prevent displacement and guide workers toward roles that complement automation. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development and places emphasis on program design, metrics and evaluation, with plans to measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to gauge the impact of automation. Funding will be provided through grants, pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The organization notes that implementation choices will determine whether the initiative yields scalable reskilling pipelines or a patchwork of pilot projects. Viewed as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative, the foundation hopes to catalyze new models of retraining and public-private collaboration, while contributing to policy discussions on workforce development such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It anticipates a rising demand for data-driven evaluations in the skills and labor analytics ecosystem as the program unfolds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES HOMETOWN FUND AIMED AT DRIVING COMMUNITY RENEWAL IN ROSEBURG

ROSEBURG, Ore. – The Ford Foundation unveiled the Hometown Fund, a $10 million, decade-long effort to revitalize Roseburg, offering capital grants up to $1 million and planning grants up to $75,000 to support large, community-driven projects and cross-sector collaboration.“So I really, really hope that the grown-ups can work together to build tangible spaces and places where kids can say that they are loved and that there’s a future here,” said Kara Inae Carlisle, President and CEO of the Ford Foundation. She highlighted the goal of lasting, meaningful change over the next ten years and a commitment to helping families thrive. The initiative builds on the foundation’s broad charitable legacy of about $810 million since inception.Historically, the foundation has focused on education, early childhood development, and community economic development. Its Ford Scholars Program currently supports roughly 1,000 students annually. The foundation has also contributed to regional crisis recovery, including collaboration with the Oregon Community Foundation and Meyer Memorial Trust to distribute $10 million for rebuilding after the 2020 Labor Day wildfires.Interested organizations should start by filling out an online information form that outlines their project’s scope and impact. After submission, Ford staff will review the proposal and schedule a meeting with the applicant. Projects meeting the fund’s goals of multi-sector collaboration and large-scale transformation will be invited to submit a formal application.

+ NHRC APPLAUDS FORD FOUNDATION'S LONGSTANDING PARTNERSHIP IN NIGERIAN HUMAN RIGHTS WORK

On Apr. 28, 2026, the NHRC in Abuja recognized the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its long-running support to human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony brought together Commission executives and was attended by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu lauded the Foundation for backing efforts to expand access to justice and to strengthen the Commission's governance and operations. He noted that tackling Nigeria's human rights challenges requires sustained cooperation with development partners to translate policy into practical outcomes for citizens.Aniagolu-Okoye said the recognition underscores the Foundation's ongoing commitment to both awareness and practical justice. She emphasized that rights education must be paired with strong enforcement mechanisms, and she highlighted the Foundation's role in supporting citizens to seek remedy when rights are violated.The event underscored the value of continuing public-private collaboration, with Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, acknowledging the MOVE project and the dashboard system as key areas advanced with Foundation support.

+ SOFT POWER OF ECOWAS FIRST LADIES HERALDED AS KEY TO GBV REFORMS, FORD FOUNDATION WARNS

The Ford Foundation urged First Ladies at the ECOWAS Forum on 24th April 2026 to harness their influence to combat gender-based violence, arguing that the soft power of First Ladies can drive social change and strengthen accountability.It highlighted the continued prevalence of GBV, noting that one in three women globally are affected and that child marriage remains a concern in West and Central Africa, with many girls married before 18.Economic costs were cited as a compelling reason for action, with GBV-related losses reaching up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, surpassing investments in some priority sectors such as education.While First Ladies are not formal policymakers, the Ford Foundation stressed their essential role in shaping public discourse and decision-making, pointing to Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as leaders whose campaigns elevated women’s issues and prompted national responses. Such examples demonstrate how strategic use of soft power can catalyze systemic change.The Foundation reiterated ongoing investments in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, including partnerships with First Ladies and governors’ spouses to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based services, and challenge harmful norms. It emphasized that laws alone are insufficient without public trust and accessible support for survivors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHN-7S43-SK5D-338C-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:11ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH USD 180 MILLION PREFERRED-NOTE FINANCING

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is designed to support the construction of the land-based facility and to help meet growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores Fortress Investment Group’s longstanding presence in Japan and its willingness to provide both capital and strategic guidance to Pure Salmon Japan. The closing signals strong institutional backing for the project and aligns with Fortress’s broader focus on sustainable infrastructure.The project targets a production capacity of 10,000 tonnes and aims to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will use advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to minimize imports and bolster food security.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress officials also noted the value of the partnership; Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, called the investment a meaningful step in strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure. As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan intends to pursue additional long-term equity partners to enhance operations.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST DEAL, AGEWELL TO OPERATE AS PART OF GROWTH IN SENIOR LIVING PORTFOLIO

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida. The transaction, funded by Fortress affiliates, was completed on 12th March 2026, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care.Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, serving an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast.Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, said: \"We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families.\"The acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

+ FORTRESS PROVIDES WHOLE-LOAN FINANCING FOR CITYVIEW'S 296-UNIT JASPER NEAR USC

Fortress Investment Group has closed a $96 million whole loan to Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily project in Downtown Los Angeles. The transaction, completed on Feb. 9, 2026, was arranged by Fortress affiliates as part of the firm's ongoing support for large-scale real estate financings.Jasper, owned and developed by Cityview, is located near the University of Southern California. The property offers studio, one- and two-bedroom units with high-end finishes and a full suite of amenities, including affordable housing units. The loan proceeds will refinance construction debt and facilitate leasing efforts for the newly opened community.Cityview's Damian Gancman highlighted Fortress's efficient execution and collaborative approach throughout the deal. At Fortress, Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt, noted that Jasper reflects Fortress's focus on backing high-quality, newly built multifamily assets backed by experienced sponsors with meaningful equity. Fortress Investment Group, founded in 1998, is a diversified global investment manager with about $54 billion in assets under management as of Sept. 30, 2025, pursuing credit and real estate, private equity and permanent capital investments.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ VERTIS INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST (NYSE:VIT) TARGETS UP TO ₹3,000 CRORE VIA IPO AND SECONDARY SALE

Vertis Infrastructure Trust (NYSE:VIT) plans to file a draft red herring prospectus by end May 2026 to raise up to ₹3,000 crore through a combination of primary and secondary share sales. The InvIT portfolio centers on toll and annuity road projects across India, built largely through acquisitions by KKR's Asian infrastructure fund which launched the platform in 2021 and expanded in 2024-25 with the acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The trust oversees 28 road projects across nine states, valued at ₹26,500 crore.Insiders note that the final offer size could be trimmed as the trust has limited requirements for new primary capital. The pricing and scale will reflect this cautious assessment within a competitive InvIT landscape. Investors have shown continued interest in InvITs as a way to access long-duration, infrastructure-backed yields.Recent sector activity underscores the momentum in InvIT fundraising, with NHAI raising over ₹9,000 crore through two InvIT offerings in March and Citius Transnet Investment Trust raising ₹1,105 crore in April, reinforcing demand despite market volatility. Vertis Infrastructure Trust remains focused on executing a disciplined and scalable IPO strategy

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) SETS $10 BILLION BUDGET FOR AI INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) will invest $10 billion toward AI-focused power plants and data centers, a move that expands capital dedicated to AI infrastructure in the United States.On 3rd May 2026, the commitment sits within a broader industry effort, with Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta collectively directing more than $100 billion worldwide into similar projects.The investment is intended to secure long-term power agreements, giving KKR leverage in power generation as demand from AI data centers climbs.The plan also intersects geopolitics, as Canada and Germany consolidate activity to counterbalance U.S.–China AI influence; KKR's participation could influence this evolving landscape.Market signals connect the investment to Google's AI development trajectory, and analysts weigh the potential for Google's stock to rise toward $310 as the infrastructure builds out.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE: KKR) SETS SPOTLIGHT AT BANK OF AMERICA SECURITIES CONFERENCE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) will have its Chief Financial Officer Robert H. Lewin present at the Bank of America Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on Feb. 10, 2026 at 2:40 PM ET. The presentation will be delivered as part of the conference program. The event will be accessible through a live webcast in KKR's Investor Center, enabling real-time participation by investors worldwide, with a replay posted shortly after the session. KKR is a global investment firm that focuses on alternative asset management, capital markets, and insurance solutions. Its portfolio spans private equity, credit, real assets, and hedge fund partnerships, and its insurance arm operates through Global Atlantic Financial Group, offering retirement, life, and reinsurance products. For additional information on KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and its strategies, visit the company’s website. Investor Relations contact details are provided there.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) EXPANDS HEALTHCARE REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT CAPABILITIES WITH KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM DEAL

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, expanding its healthcare portfolio. The terms of the deal are not disclosed in a release dated May 4, 2026. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the new RCM platform, aligning with Carlyle's plan to fuse Knack's analytics with Rao's global operations to bolster advisory servicesKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM count physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals among their clients. The collaboration targets rural cost reports and complex anesthesia cases, as noted by Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle frames the move as a platform for broader participation in the RCM market and signals intent to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementCarlyle sets its course to strengthen capabilities in healthcare management and revenue cycle technology, with the expectation of delivering innovative solutions that support providers in navigating a changing healthcare landscape

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) UNITES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO EXPAND DIGITAL HEALTHCARE SERVICES PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is integrating Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a unified platform designed to boost digital capabilities across healthcare services. The move addresses ongoing structural changes in the U.S. healthcare system, including administrative complexities, workforce challenges, and evolving care delivery models. The combined platform will support physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers requiring tailored operational assistance, leveraging Knack's globally distributed operational framework and Equalize's tools for predictive analytics and optimization. The integration aims to advance revenue cycle management by enhancing billing, claims processing, and payer coordination, potentially improving efficiency and regulatory compliance. The initiative aligns with market trends as providers seek technology-driven solutions to navigate operational complexities, and Carlyle positions itself to participate in a sector undergoing substantial transformation.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) NAMES THREE CO-PRESIDENTS TO SHARPEN EXECUTION AND STRATEGY

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective 22nd April 2026, to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as the firm refines its strategy in a slower macro environment.The appointments signal a structural shift intended to sharpen execution across investment, fundraising, and operational priorities at Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) as rates rise and deal activity slows.Schwartz has emphasized clearer accountability and faster decisions since taking the helm, and the co-presidents model mirrors arrangements seen at other large private equity firms to share oversight of key levers.Investors will watch how the changes influence Carlyle Group's investment activity and quarterly results in the quarters ahead.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL PROVIDES $300 MILLION FINANCING TO ESTABLISHMENT LABS

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan from Oaktree Capital Management. The facility, finalized on 3rd May 2026, refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives.The loan carries a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure with flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, enhancing funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues its operational and expansion plans.The investment thesis centers on the Motiva implant platform and minimally invasive procedures expected to drive broad adoption.Management guidance for 2026 projects revenue of US$264-266 million, implying roughly 25-26% growth from the prior year. The financing should ease refinancing pressure and provide liquidity to address losses and cash burn without an immediate equity raise.While the capital raise improves flexibility, there are risks linked to ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts call for around 30% revenue growth with earnings near US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could reinforce or temper optimism about profitability. The agreement marks a strategic shift in its financing approach.

+ BIOXCEL AND OAKTREE EXTEND REGISTRATION RIGHTS, ISSUE PRIVATE WARRANTS

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed in an 8-K filing that it has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and a group of institutional holders. The move aligns with the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and provides a framework for potential future equity transactions under the arrangement disclosed on 18 April 2026. Key elements of the deal include amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was signed on 27 March 2026, setting the stage for these warrants. On 15 April 2026, the company issued the unregistered warrants in a private placement, a mechanism that avoided immediate Securities Act registration as part of the financing strategy. BioXcel said it would file registration statements with the SEC to enable possible resale of the warrants' underlying shares, a move designed to improve liquidity and maintain investor confidence. Lenders have indicated they intend to hold the securities for investment purposes rather than seek rapid resale, which could moderate near-term volatility in the stock price. Market participants should note the potential dilution risk if warrants are exercised, and the registration process could increase the total share count. The financing approach signals the company's liquidity needs and strategic objectives, rather than an implicit endorsement of aggressive share issuance. BioXcel also clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting and regulatory requirements.

+ OAKTREE PARTNERS WITH NORTH MILL ON $355.8 MILLION EQUIPMENT FINANCE SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management, in partnership with North Mill Equipment Finance, has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization called OAKEF 2026-1. The closing occurred on 8th April 2026 and signals strong investor appetite for quality equipment finance assets.The collateral consists of equipment finance assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal completed in November 2025.The offering was rated from AAA to single A and attracted peak subscriptions of approximately 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack. The deal delivered a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%.Truist Securities served as the lead structuring agent, with Deutsche Bank acting as co-manager.Brendan Beer, portfolio manager for Oaktree's asset-backed finance and structured credit platform, said the market response reflected investor confidence in Oaktree's equipment strategy and highlighted the North Mill collaboration as a key driver.David Lee, chairman and CEO of North Mill Equipment Finance, stressed that the partnership demonstrates asset quality and the servicing platform's strength, noting that NMEF will continue as primary servicer for the assets.The securitization reinforces Oaktree's position in asset-backed finance and shows its ability to attract significant investor interest and structure deals that meet partner financing objectives.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION INVESTMENT FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT (NYSE:APO) THROUGH CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED SHARES

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., through the issuance of convertible preferred shares that grant Apollo a 13% ownership stake. The transaction was formalized on April 20, 2026 and remains subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The deal values the company at a post-money of approximately $9.62 billion, with the new capital intended to enhance operational capabilities and broaden market reach.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) POSTS 13% MINORITY STAKE IN MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS VIA $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The move aligns with McKesson's strategy to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and provides capital to advance strategic initiatives. The convertible preferred structure allows Apollo to participate in potential upside while McKesson maintains a staged path toward the separation. The investment underscores a strategic alignment between the two firms as McKesson seeks to optimize its core businesses. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, signaling investor confidence in growth opportunities. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells and one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will monitor the implications for McKesson's separation plans and for Apollo's portfolio strategy.

+ APOLLO CLOSES GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP DEAL TO BOLSTER UK BUILD-TO-RENT PLATFORM

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK housing footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a transaction completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed.The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans.GLG is recognized as a leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired more than 5,000 homes.Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios and helping GLG access capital from both Apollo and third-party partners to accelerate development.Gatehouse Bank said the sale will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) REPORTS $53.0 BILLION OF U.S. DIRECT LENDING CLOSED ACROSS 348 TRANSACTIONS OVER THE 12 MONTHS THROUGH MARCH 31, 2026

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update on the 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors and highlights the breadth of the platform.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, the firm Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, supported Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's credit facility backing the North American Science Association.As of March 31, 2026, the global platform Totals more than $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) RAISES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND; FIRST CLOSE EXPECTED IN SPRING

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting ongoing investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The fund’s first close is expected to occur in spring, per remarks from Chief Executive Michael Arougheti on an earnings callArougheti described the fund as having "very strong demand" after the solid performance of prior vintages. The fundraising comes more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalBeyond Japan, Ares has advanced other capital initiatives, including Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets that include a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, said the firm is fundraising to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, largely tied to commitments to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VIIReturning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is reviewing a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account sharing a mandate similar to the SSG series, focused on non performing loans and single-asset distress in Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) EXPANDS RETAIL PLATFORM WITH US$1.7 BILLION WHITESTONE REIT PURCHASE

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has agreed to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding its footprint in necessity-based retail centers across major U.S. metro areas. The deal signals progress in Ares' strategy to deepen exposure to essential consumer needs within real estate and related assets. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, and appointed Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong to lead the Asia Special Situations team, reflecting the firm's push to grow credit and special situations in Asia. Whitestone's inclusion strengthens Ares' position in retail real estate and aligns with a broader plan to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types, including private credit and real assets. Investors will assess how Whitestone integrates with Ares' existing operations and the progress of Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape. The deal introduces potential risks such as profit margin compression and higher leverage, while the dividend yield of 5.38% is not fully supported by earnings

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO REOPENS DOHA–BAGHDAD FREIGHT LINK WITH HIGHER CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, reestablishing the Doha to Baghdad cargo link. Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will launch a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad, paired with twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route from 10th May to provide additional belly-hold capacity.The combined cargo capacity is set to exceed 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad from 10 May, reflecting an integrated approach that blends dedicated freighter uplift with passenger capacity.The move comes as Qatar Airways Cargo continues scaling its network. The freighter network now reaches more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while the passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June. Bookings are open via Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales channels. For shipments, contact cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or your local representative. The resumption sits within a broader network strategy, supported by a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, and a road feeder network as the airline continues its investments in digitalization, products, and infrastructure.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES ERBIL ROUTE IN PHASE, EXPANDS IRAQI CONNECTIVITY

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the carrier 's return to direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a phased reinstatement of its Iraq operations. The airline will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW) and Basra (BSR) — in a staged rollout aimed at rebuilding its regional network and reconnecting key Middle Eastern destinations.Freighter operations to Baghdad will resume on 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq and supporting broader network restoration. These resumptions follow earlier reinstatements to Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus and Kozhikode as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, the carrier expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide, with Erbil travellers gaining access to the Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a pivotal connector linking six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS INCREASES DOHA–AUSTRALIA FLIGHTS, DRIVING TOURISM AND TRADE MOMENTUM

Qatar Airways announced a substantial expansion of its Doha–Australia network, with daily Doha–Sydney, Doha–Melbourne and Doha–Perth services resuming from May 2026. Brisbane will see four weekly flights from 16 May, increasing to daily by 16 June 2026, expanding access to Queensland’s attractions.The changes mark a strategic use of the airline’s Doha hub to re-link Europe, the Middle East and Asia with Australia, aiding the country’s tourism rebound after disruption.The industry effects are expected to be significant: increased international visitors should boost demand for accommodations, tours and attractions in Sydney, Melbourne, Perth, and Brisbane, particularly ahead of the summer travel season. The expansion also has implications for trade and business, with more direct connections between Australia and its partner regions.Travel advisories continue to apply; Do Not Travel advisory for Qatar remains, including transit through Hamad International Airport. Yet airline officials emphasize confidence in safety and operational reliability, keeping faith in the long term tourism partnership.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-KD23-RXGW-R28P-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS WITH PEER COMPARISONS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS WITH PEER COMPARISONS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [4 Press Releases]

+ WRI & ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION: EARLY CLIMATE HEALTH INVESTMENTS GENERATE 68-FOLD GAINS IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

New World Resources Institute analysis, supported by The Rockefeller Foundation, reveals that every $1 invested in preparing for climate-caused health risks can yield up to $68 in benefits for communities in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Middle East

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION DENGUEAI: NEW MODEL THAT ANTICIPATES OUTBREAKS WITH 93% EFFECTIVENESS AND THREE WEEKS ADVANCE NOTICE

Cali, Colombia | May 4, 2026 - The City of Santiago de Cali, Universidad Icesi, and The Rockefeller Foundation today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) aimed at reducing the incidence of dengue in the city and strengthening preparedness and response capacities for future epidemics.

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: May 01, 2026: Milbank Memorial Fund Seeks Applicants for Leadership Development in Health Policy

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-KSM3-S0RF-V4GV-00000-00May 01, 2026: Milbank Memorial Fund Seeks Applicants for Leadership Development in Health Policy2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:10ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 01, 2026: Milbank Memorial Fund Seeks Applicants for Leadership Development in Health Policy

On the 1st of May, 2026, the Milbank Memorial Fund opened applications for the 2026-2027 cohorts of its Milbank Fellows Program and Emerging Leaders Program.

The window runs through June 19th, 2026, with the MFP serving senior state government leaders and the ELP targeting rising leaders in state, large-county, and large-city governments.

All program costs are covered, and participants will collaborate with peers from diverse regions. The curriculum centers on leadership development, health policy topics, and the ability to implement impactful health policies locally.

Selection emphasizes a commitment to health improvement and reducing disparities; candidates should hold influential roles and be willing to share expertise on population health policy. Interested officials may apply or nominate someone who meets these qualifications at the Milbank website.

INDEX

SECTION 1 MILBANK LLP PROFILE

SECTION 2 MILBANK LLP TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 MILBANK LLP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

Milbank LLP's mission, vision, and values are centered around delivering exceptional results and fostering an inclusive environment. Here's a brief overview:

Mission

Milbank LLP is driven to deliver exceptional results for their clients by pushing boundaries and challenging assumptions. They aim to provide tailored solutions that exceed their clients' highest expectations.

Vision

The firm envisions itself as a leader in the legal industry, shaping the legal landscape and working with market leaders, global innovators, and paradigm-shifting entrepreneurs.

Values

Milbank LLP values excellence, diversity, and inclusion. They celebrate individuality and are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels valued1. The firm also emphasizes professional development and building lasting relationships through various affinity groups and mentoring programs.

1.2 SUMMARY

urn:contentItem:6JHK-F5B3-RRV5-436X-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [4 Press Releases]

+ WRI & ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION: EARLY CLIMATE HEALTH INVESTMENTS GENERATE 68-FOLD GAINS IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

New World Resources Institute analysis, supported by The Rockefeller Foundation, reveals that every $1 invested in preparing for climate-caused health risks can yield up to $68 in benefits for communities in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Middle East

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION DENGUEAI: NEW MODEL THAT ANTICIPATES OUTBREAKS WITH 93% EFFECTIVENESS AND THREE WEEKS ADVANCE NOTICE

Cali, Colombia | May 4, 2026 - The City of Santiago de Cali, Universidad Icesi, and The Rockefeller Foundation today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) aimed at reducing the incidence of dengue in the city and strengthening preparedness and response capacities for future epidemics.

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Launches Big Bets Fellowship to Transform Africa

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-F5B3-RRV5-4303-00000-00April 28, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Launches Big Bets Fellowship to Transform Africa2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:10ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Launches Big Bets Fellowship to Transform Africa

The Rockefeller Foundation has launched the inaugural class of Africa Big Bets Fellows during its AfricaXchange convening in Nairobi, Kenya. The first cohort includes 10 changemakers from Ghana, Kenya, Malawi, Nigeria, South Africa, and Tanzania, selected to lead innovative, community-driven solutions addressing key development challenges across the continent. The fellowship will run for five months and aims to help scale impactful ideas in areas such as energy access, food security, healthcare, financial inclusion, climate resilience, and migration.

This initiative coincides with the 28th of April, 2026, marking the 60th anniversary of the Rockefeller Foundation’s Africa Regional Office and reflects its long-standing engagement in the continent’s development. Leaders from the Foundation emphasized that the fellowship supports a new generation of African innovators whose locally driven solutions are designed to expand opportunity, strengthen resilience, and improve quality of life across diverse sectors including health, agriculture, clean energy, and digital finance.

Across Africa, the program comes at a time when major structural challenges persist, including limited access to electricity for hundreds of millions of people and significant gaps in global climate finance. Despite these challenges, Africa holds vast potential for renewable energy development and sustainable growth, with projections indicating that a large share of future energy demand could be met through clean energy sources if adequately supported.

The selected fellows are working on a wide range of solutions, including mobile healthcare services in rural Ghana, waste-to-resource systems for cleaner cities, regenerative agriculture linked to school feeding programs in Kenya, AI-powered farming innovation in Malawi, and improved access to financial services in Nigeria. Other projects focus on clean energy access for off-grid communities, climate-resilient farming, youth-led media platforms, and real-time migration and climate information systems.

The fellowship is part of a broader AfricaXchange platform that brings together funders, practitioners, and policymakers to strengthen locally led development. It reflects a growing emphasis on ensuring that African solutions are designed, financed, and led within the continent, while also connecting innovators to global networks and resources to scale their impact. The Rockefeller Foundation highlighted that this initiative builds on more than a century of work in Africa, continuing its legacy by supporting emerging leaders whose ideas aim to deliver measurable, scalable, and sustainable impact across the continent and beyond.

In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. urn:contentItem:6JHM-XJ53-RSYN-92SP-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [4 Press Releases]

+ WRI & ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION: EARLY CLIMATE HEALTH INVESTMENTS GENERATE 68-FOLD GAINS IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

New World Resources Institute analysis, supported by The Rockefeller Foundation, reveals that every $1 invested in preparing for climate-caused health risks can yield up to $68 in benefits for communities in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Middle East

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION DENGUEAI: NEW MODEL THAT ANTICIPATES OUTBREAKS WITH 93% EFFECTIVENESS AND THREE WEEKS ADVANCE NOTICE

Cali, Colombia | May 4, 2026 - The City of Santiago de Cali, Universidad Icesi, and The Rockefeller Foundation today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) aimed at reducing the incidence of dengue in the city and strengthening preparedness and response capacities for future epidemics.

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
